module-pool-024.easyUnderstanding: Simple words mein samjho: Module Pool transport karna matlab ek ghar shift karna — sirf sofa nahi, poora furniture saath le jaana padta hai. Agar module pool program transport kiya aur screens bhool gaye — transaction chalega hi nahi destination system mein. Transport mein kya saath jaana chahiye: SAPMZ_VENDOR program (main body), screen includes SAPMZ_VENDOR_O01 (PBO screen 100), SAPMZ_VENDOR_I01 (PAI screen 100), SAPMZ_VENDOR_O02, SAPMZ_VENDOR_I02, GUI Status SAPMZVENDOR_N (SE41 mein banaya menu painter object), T-code entry SE93 mein jo transaction launch karta hai. Ek bhi cheez chhoot gayi toh target system mein error aayega — 'Program not found' ya 'Screen not found'. Isliye module pool transport karte waqt complete package bhejte hain, ek ek object alag nahi.
module-pool-024.hinglishMasterExplanation:
Module Pool transport ek multi-object operation hai — program, screens, GUI status, aur T-code sab alag-alag SAP objects hain, sab ek saath transport karne chahiye.

Samjho kya-kya transport hota hai:

1. Main Program (SAPMZ*): Ye module pool program hai — PBO/PAI modules, FORM routines, INCLUDE files. ABAP program object hai — R3TR PROG.

2. Screen Includes (SAPMZ*O01, SAPMZ*I01): Har screen ke liye do includes hote hain — O (Output = PBO) aur I (Input = PAI) per screen number. Screen 100: SAPMZ*O01 + SAPMZ*I01. Screen 200: SAPMZ*O02 + SAPMZ*I02. Ye automatically main program ke saath include hote hain jab SE80 ya SE38 se transport request create karte hain.

3. Dynpro (Screen): Screen Painter (SE51) mein design kiya gaya screen — field layout, attributes, flow logic. Object type R3TR DYNP.

4. GUI Status (Menu Painter): SE41 mein banaya GUI Status (toolbar, menu, function codes). Object type R3TR MESS (ya GUISTATUS depending on SAP release).

5. T-code (SE93): Transaction code jo module pool ka starting screen point karta hai. Object type R3TR TRAN.

Transport request check karna:
- SE80 mein program open karo → Transport request button → check kaunse objects included hain.
- SE93 mein T-code open karo → Request/Task → check karta hai ki T-code transport request mein hai ya nahi.
- STMS mein transport request status check karo.

Safe transport sequence:
1. Development mein complete testing karo — sab screens, sab buttons, sab navigation paths.
2. ABAP Test Cockpit (ATC) check run karo — syntax errors aur warnings pakdo.
3. Transport request mein sab objects add hain confirm karo.
4. Quality system mein import karo, regression test karo.
5. Production mein only after QA sign-off transport karo.

Transport risk mitigation:
- Module pool screens tight coupling hoti hain — ek screen galat import ho toh poora transaction fail.
- Always transport complete package — kabhi ek program alag aur screen alag import nahi karo.
module-pool-024.interviewMeKyaBolnaHai:
Interview Me Bolna Hai:
'To transport a module pool safely, I include the complete package in the transport request: the main SAPMZ program, all screen includes (O01, I01 per screen), Dynpro screens from SE51, the GUI Status from SE41 Menu Painter, and the T-code entry from SE93. I verify the transport request contents in SE80 before releasing. I also run ATC checks to catch any syntax or quality issues. In STMS, I transport to Quality first, run regression testing, and only move to Production after sign-off. Partial transport — program without screens or T-code — will cause runtime errors in the target system.'

Hinglish tip: 'Transport question mein concrete object names bolo — SAPMZ*O01, SAPMZ*I01, SE41, SE93, STMS. Ye dikhata hai ki tum actual transport process se familiar ho, sirf theory nahi. Ek real gotcha bhi bataao: ek baar screen transport bhool gaye the Quality mein — transaction chalaya toh Screen not found dump aaya. Tab complete checklist banaya. Ye story professional maturity dikhati hai.'

commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Production impact: runtime dump visible in ST22.
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Use the correct approach as described in the deep-dive section.

commonMistakesSection.mistake: T-code transport request mein add karna bhool jaana — QA mein program aur screens import hue, T-code nahi.
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Production impact: incorrect results or wasted effort that shows up only in QA/UAT.
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: QA mein SE93 manually banana pada. Production mein same issue repeat — proper transport process follow karo.

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Development mein testing skip karke seedha QA transport karna — QA is for validation, not for development debugging.
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Production impact: incorrect results or wasted effort that shows up only in QA/UAT.
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Basic bugs dev mein pakdo, QA mein business scenarios validate karo.

module-pool-024.quickRevisionNotes:

Used for:

- Audit-ready transport documentation

followupAnswerBank.question: What objects are included when you transport a Module Pool program?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Ek complete module pool transport include karta hai: main program (R3TR PROG), Dynpro screens (R3TR DYNP), Menu Painter se GUI Status (R3TR CUAD), aur SE93 se transaction code (R3TR TRAN). PBO aur PAI ke liye screen includes automatically main program ka part hote hain. Main release karne se pehle SE09 ke through verify karta hoon ki transport request mein saare object types present hain.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: A complete module pool transport includes: the main program (R3TR PROG), Dynpro screens (R3TR DYNP), GUI Status from Menu Painter (R3TR CUAD), and the transaction code from SE93 (R3TR TRAN). Screen includes for PBO and PAI are automatically part of the main program. I verify all object types are present in the transport request via SE09 before releasing.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Transport request verify karne ka quick way: SE09 open karo, request number enter karo, objects tab mein dekho. Har object type ka prefix hota hai — R3TR PROG, R3TR DYNP, R3TR TRAN. Agar koi missing hai toh manually add karo before release.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Sirf ABAP program transport karna aur screens alag transport karna later — agar sequence galat hua toh target system mein intermediate state aata hai jahan program hai but screens nahi, causing runtime dump.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you check if a T-code is in a transport request?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: T-code transport request mein hai verify karne ke liye, main SE93 open karta hoon, change mode mein T-code enter karta hoon, aur menu Utilities → Transport request assignment check karta hoon. Alternatively, SE09 mein main transport request open karta hoon aur transaction code name ke saath object type R3TR TRAN dhundhta hoon. Agar T-code missing hai, main SE09 mein object R3TR TRAN add karke manually add karta hoon.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: To verify a T-code is in the transport request, I open SE93, enter the T-code in change mode, and check the menu Utilities → Transport request assignment. Alternatively, in SE09 I open the transport request and look for object type R3TR TRAN with the transaction code name. If the T-code is missing, I add it manually via SE09 by adding object R3TR TRAN.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Common scenario: developer banaya T-code SE93 mein, alag task pe kaam kiya, alag transport request bana gaya T-code ke liye. Merge karna padta hai — SE09 mein main request mein T-code object add karo manually. Ya dono requests ek saath transport karo — same import batch.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: T-code transport request mein add karna bhool jaana — QA mein program aur screens import hue, T-code nahi. QA mein SE93 manually banana pada. Production mein same issue repeat — proper transport process follow karo.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" SE93 — check transport assignment:
" Transaction SE93 → Enter T-code ZVD_ENTRY
" → Display mode → Utilities → Transport request

" SE09 — manually add T-code to transport request:
" SE09 → Open request ZCD110001 → Objects tab
" → Add row: R3TR | TRAN | ZVD_ENTRY
" → Save → T-code now included in transport

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the correct transport sequence for Module Pool to Production?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Safe sequence hai: DEV mein complete development testing, ATC checks run karna, SE09 mein transport request release karna, STMS ke through Quality system mein import karna, QA mein regression aur UAT testing perform karna, QA sign-off prapt karna, phir agreed change window ke dauran Production import schedule karna. Production import ke baad, main smoke test karta hoon — T-code run karke aur key flows validate karke — transport success confirm karne se pehle.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: The safe sequence is: complete development testing in DEV, run ATC checks, release the transport request in SE09, import to Quality system via STMS, perform regression and UAT testing in QA, obtain QA sign-off, then schedule the Production import during the agreed change window. After Production import, I perform a smoke test — running the T-code and validating key flows — before confirming the transport success.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Production transport best practices: weekend maintenance window prefer karo for module pool — users nahi hote. Import karne ke baad immediately T-code chalao aur basic create-save flow test karo. Agar kuch issue aaya — hotfix transport ready rakho ya rollback plan batao before import.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Development mein testing skip karke seedha QA transport karna — QA is for validation, not for development debugging. Basic bugs dev mein pakdo, QA mein business scenarios validate karo.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Transport flow:
" DEV → (SE09 Release) → QA → (QA sign-off) → PRD

" STMS import steps:
" STMS → Import Overview → Select target system (QA)
" → Select transport request → Import
" → Monitor import log for errors (green = ok, red = error)

" After Production import — smoke test:
" Run T-code ZVD_ENTRY → check screen loads
" Enter test data → validate screen 100 → screen 200
" Save → check database record created

followupAnswerBank.question: What happens if you transport screens without transporting the GUI Status?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Agar screens GUI Status transport kiye bina transported hain, PBO module SET PF-STATUS ko aise status name ke saath call karta hai jo target system mein exist nahi karta. Yeh screen ko bina koi toolbar ya menu ke display hone ka cause hota hai — koi buttons available nahi hain. User effectively screen pe stuck hota hai bina save, navigate, ya exit karne ke koi tarika ke. Fix transport request mein GUI Status object (R3TR CUAD) include karna aur re-transport karna hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: If screens are transported without the GUI Status, the PBO module calls SET PF-STATUS with a status name that does not exist in the target system. This causes the screen to display without any toolbar or menu — no buttons are available. The user is effectively stuck on the screen with no way to save, navigate, or exit. The fix is to include the GUI Status object (R3TR CUAD) in the transport request and re-transport.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Production issue example: urgent transport kiya screen changes ka, GUI Status transport request mein tha nahi. Users ne T-code chalaya — screen aaya but blank toolbar. Support call aaya. Emergency transport kiya GUI Status ka. 2 ghante downtime effectively. Isliye checklist follow karo har transport pe.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: GUI Status transport sirf ek baar zaroorat hai — pehli deployment pe. But agar GUI Status mein koi change kiya (naaya button add kiya) toh dobara transport zaroori hai. Change track karo.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Symptom of missing GUI Status transport:
" Screen opens but toolbar is completely blank
" No Save/Back/Exit buttons visible
" User cannot perform any action

" Fix: add GUI Status to transport request
" SE41 → Open GUI Status 'ZSTATUS_MAIN'
"       → Program name: SAPMZ_VENDOR_DIALOG
" → Utilities → Transport → Add to request ZCD110001

" Prevention: checklist in SE09 before release:
" Check R3TR CUAD SAPMZ_VENDOR_DIALOG is present

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you handle a failed module pool transport in Production?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Jab ek Production transport fail hota hai, main pehle specific object aur error identify karne ke liye STMS import log check karta hoon. Agar failure ek syntax error hai, main development mein fix karta hoon aur ek new transport request create karta hoon. Agar import ne system ko ek partial state mein chhod diya — new program but old screens — main business impact assess karta hoon. Critical issues ke liye main last good state restore karne ke liye previous working transport re-import karta hoon. Main kabhi Production mein objects manually edit nahi karta — saare fixes proper transport ke through jaate hain.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: When a Production transport fails, I first check the STMS import log to identify the specific object and error. If the failure is a syntax error, I fix it in development and create a new transport request. If the import left the system in a partial state — new program but old screens — I assess business impact. For critical issues I re-import the previous working transport to restore the last good state. I never manually edit objects in Production — all fixes go through proper transport.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Rollback strategy: keep note of the previous transport request number before every Production import. Agar nayi transport fail hui, toh previous request ko STMS se re-import karo — ye rollback hai. This is why change management records all transport numbers with timestamps.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Production mein directly SE38 khol ke code fix karna — ye transport bypass karta hai. Next transport se production ka code overwritten ho jaata hai. Client audit mein bhi issue hota hai — unauthorized change.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" STMS import log check:
" STMS → Import Overview → Import history
" → Select failed request → Log → View details
" Green = success, Red = error, Yellow = warning

" Common error messages:
" 'Program SAPMZ_VENDOR_DIALOG: Syntax error'  ← Fix in DEV
" 'Screen 0100 import failed: lock conflict'   ← Retry import
" 'Authorization missing for object R3TR PROG' ← Basis team

" Rollback: re-import previous working transport
" STMS → Import → Select previous request ZCD109999
" → Import again → overrides failed import

followupAnswerBank.question: What is SE09 and how do you use it for Module Pool transport management?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: SE09 Transport Organizer hai jahaan main saare transport requests manage karta hoon. Main isse use karta hoon request contents view karne, missing objects add karne, aur tasks aur requests release karne ke liye. Module pool ke liye, main Workbench Request create karta hoon kyunki yeh ABAP program objects handle karta hai. Release karne se pehle, main verify karta hoon ki saare objects — program, screens, GUI status, T-code — listed hain. Development complete aur tested hone ke baad, main task pehle release karta hoon, phir parent request, jo isse STMS import ke through Quality mein available banata hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: SE09 is the Transport Organizer where I manage all transport requests. I use it to view request contents, add missing objects, and release tasks and requests. For module pool, I create a Workbench Request since it handles ABAP program objects. Before releasing, I verify all objects — program, screens, GUI status, T-code — are listed. After development is complete and tested, I release the task first, then the parent request, which makes it available for STMS import into Quality.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: SE09 workflow: developer creates task, codes module pool, objects auto-added to task when saved. Before release: open SE09, verify objects, check for any manually added objects needed. Release task → project lead reviews → releases parent request → Basis imports via STMS.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Customizing Request mein module pool program add karna — galat request type. Customizing requests sirf table entries ke liye (IMG settings). ABAP programs aur screens Workbench Requests mein jaate hain. Wrong type se import issues aa sakte hain.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" SE09 navigation:
" SE09 → My requests → Workbench requests
" → Expand request → Expand task
" → Objects tab shows all included objects

" Release sequence:
" 1. Release Task: right-click task → Release
" 2. Release Request: right-click parent request → Release
"    (Only after all tasks released)
" 3. Basis imports via STMS to QA

" Add missing object manually in SE09:
" Open request → Objects → Add row:
" Object type: R3TR, Object: DYNP
" Sub-object: SAPMZ_VENDOR_DIALOG 0100

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you ensure module pool transport does not break existing functionality in Production?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Production mein transport karne se pehle, main Quality mein ek structured regression test run karta hoon jo saare screens correctly open hone, Back aur Cancel sahit saare navigation paths, saare function key handlers, mandatory field validations correct messages trigger karne, aur database verification ke saath end-to-end save flow cover karta hai. Main different user authorizations ke saath bhi test karta hoon ensure karne ke liye ki auth checks kaam karte hain. Is checklist pe QA sign-off ke baad hi main Production transport approve karta hoon.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Before transporting to Production, I run a structured regression test in Quality covering all screens opening correctly, all navigation paths including Back and Cancel, all function key handlers, mandatory field validations triggering correct messages, and end-to-end save flow with database verification. I also test with different user authorizations to ensure auth checks work. Only after QA sign-off on this checklist do I approve the Production transport.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Practical tip: module pool regression mein screen recording tool use karo (SAP GUI scripting) to record happy-path test and replay it after each transport. Automation saves time especially for 5+ screen module pools. Manual testing ke saath automated smoke test — best coverage.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Sirf happy path test karna — data enter karo, save karo, done. Edge cases miss ho jaate hain: empty mandatory fields, invalid data, Cancel mid-way through, Back from screen 200 to 100. These are common production issues.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" QA Regression Test Checklist for Module Pool:
" 1. T-code runs → Screen 100 opens correctly
" 2. Screen 100: all fields editable as expected
" 3. Enter without data → mandatory field error shown
" 4. Enter valid data → navigates to Screen 200
" 5. Screen 200: table control shows with correct columns
" 6. Add row data → SAVE → confirmation message
" 7. Database: record exists in correct Z-table
" 8. Back from Screen 200 → Screen 100 → Exit
" 9. Cancel at any point → clean exit, no DB change
" 10. Unauthorized user → auth error message + exit

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the difference between Workbench Request and Customizing Request in transport?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Ek Workbench Request cross-client ABAP development objects ke liye hai — programs, classes, screens, function modules, data dictionary objects. Module pool programs aur screens Workbench Requests mein jaate hain. Ek Customizing Request client-specific hai aur IMG settings aur table entries jaise configuration data ke liye use hota hai. Inhe mix karne se import issues hote hain — Customizing Request mein ek module pool target client mein correctly import nahi ho sakta.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: A Workbench Request is for cross-client ABAP development objects — programs, classes, screens, function modules, data dictionary objects. Module pool programs and screens go into Workbench Requests. A Customizing Request is client-specific and used for configuration data like IMG settings and table entries. Mixing them causes import issues — a module pool in a Customizing Request may not import correctly to the target client.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: When creating a module pool program in SE38: first save pop-up asks which transport request. Always select or create a Workbench Request — never use Customizing for program code. Similarly SE93 T-code creation creates a Workbench transport entry automatically.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Workbench mein program, Customizing mein T-code separately — possible but transport management nightmare. Keep all module pool objects in the same Workbench Request for atomic transport.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" When SE38 prompts for transport request on first save:
" Choose: Workbench request (not Customizing)
" Request type visible in SE09:
"   'K' prefix = Workbench Request (Korrektur)
"   'C' prefix = Customizing Request

" Example transport numbers:
" DEVK900123 = Workbench (K) → use for module pool
" DEVC900456 = Customizing (C) → use for table entries

" SE09 filter to see only Workbench:
" SE09 → Tasks and requests → Workbench requests (filter)

module-pool-025.easyUnderstanding: Simple words mein samjho: Interview mein 'Module Pool experience bataao' poocha gaya toh ek real project example dena best approach hai — sirf theory nahi. Jo answer zyada impactful hota hai: 'Maine ek custom Material Movement dialog transaction banaya using Module Pool — 2 screens the, screen 100 mein header info tha (movement type, plant, posting date), screen 200 mein table control tha batch-wise entry ke liye. Sab function keys handle kiye OKCODE pattern se, T-code SE93 se create kiya, complete package transport kiya saath — sab screen includes, GUI status, T-code sab. Production mein successfully use ho raha hai.' Ye answer strong hai kyunki: ek specific use case hai, technical details hain (table control, OKCODE, SE93), aur end-to-end delivery ka mention hai (transport). Vague answers jaise 'haan module pool pata hai, screens banate hain' — ye impress nahi karta.
module-pool-025.hinglishMasterExplanation:
Practical interview answer ke liye ek well-crafted project story ready rakhna chahiye. Story mein yeh elements hone chahiye:

1. Business requirement: kya banana tha — custom transaction for what business process.

2. Technical architecture: kitni screens, kya kya tha har screen mein, table control tha kya, navigation flow kya tha.

3. Key implementation points: OKCODE pattern, PBO/PAI events, GUI Status in SE41, validations kya lagayi.

4. T-code creation: SE93 mein dialog transaction type, program name, starting screen.

5. Transport: complete package — program, screen includes, GUI status, T-code — sab ek saath transport kiya.

6. Result: users kya use kar rahe hain, koi specific business value.

Best project example: Custom Material Movement Dialog Transaction — ek common, relatable use case jo almost har company mein hoti hai. 2 screens banao story mein: Screen 100 = movement header (movement type, plant, posting date), Screen 200 = table control for batch-wise material entry (material number, quantity, batch, storage location).

PAI mein kya kiya: OKCODE check, mandatory validation (movement type mandatory), table control row data capture, BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE ya direct MKPF/MSEG insert.

GUI Status: SE41 mein banaya with function keys F5 = Add Row, F8 = Post, Escape = Cancel.

SE93: dialog transaction, SAPMZ_MAT_MOVE, screen 100.

Transport: complete package — SAPMZ_MAT_MOVE (program), SAPMZ_MAT_MOVE_O01/I01 (screen 100 includes), SAPMZ_MAT_MOVE_O02/I02 (screen 200), GUI status SAPMZ_MAT_MOVE_N, T-code ZMMAT_MOVE.

Agar tum yeh answer dete ho — interviewer ko pata chalta hai ki real module pool development tumne kiya hai.
module-pool-025.interviewMeKyaBolnaHai:
Interview Me Bolna Hai:
'I developed a custom Material Movement dialog transaction using Module Pool. The requirement was to allow warehouse users to post batch material movements with multiple line items in a single transaction — standard MIGO was too complex for this specific use case. I built two screens: Screen 100 for header data with movement type, plant, and posting date; Screen 200 with a table control for batch-wise material entry including material number, quantity, batch, and storage location. I handled all function keys using the OKCODE pattern in PAI — F5 to add a new row to the table control, F8 to validate and post, and standard Back/Cancel for navigation. I created the T-code in SE93 as a dialog transaction type pointing to program SAPMZ_MAT_MOVE Screen 100. For transport, I packaged everything together: the main program, all screen includes (O01, I01, O02, I02), the GUI status from Menu Painter, and the T-code — all in the same Workbench transport request. The transaction has been in Production for 18 months and is used daily by 20 warehouse users.'

Hinglish tip: 'Numbers dena powerful hota hai — 2 screens, 20 users, 18 months in production. Ye specificity credibility badhata hai. Vague answers avoid karo. Ek project clearly describe karna 5 vague examples se better hai.'
module-pool-025.codeExamples:
PROGRAM sapmz_mat_move MESSAGE-ID zmsg.

TABLES: mkpf.
DATA: ok_code   TYPE sy-ucomm,
      gv_werks  TYPE werks_d,
      gv_bwart  TYPE bwart,
      gv_budat  TYPE budat.

TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_item,
         matnr TYPE matnr,
         menge TYPE menge_d,
         charg TYPE charg_d,
         lgort TYPE lgort_d,
END OF ty_item.
DATA: lt_items  TYPE TABLE OF ty_item,
      ls_item   TYPE ty_item.

" Table control definition
CONTROLS: tc_items TYPE TABLEVIEW USING SCREEN 0200.

MODULE pbo_0100 OUTPUT.
  SET PF-STATUS 'ZSTATUS_HDR'.
  SET TITLEBAR 'ZTITLE_100' WITH 'Material Movement Entry'.
ENDMODULE.

MODULE pai_0100 INPUT.
  SAVE_OK_CODE ok_code.
  CASE ok_code.
    WHEN 'NEXT'.
      IF gv_bwart IS INITIAL.
        MESSAGE e001(zmsg) WITH 'Movement Type is mandatory'.
      ELSE.
        SET SCREEN 200. LEAVE SCREEN.
      ENDIF.
    WHEN 'BACK' OR 'CANCEL'. SET SCREEN 0. LEAVE SCREEN.
  ENDCASE.
ENDMODULE.
module-pool-025.interviewScriptEnglish: I developed a custom Material Movement dialog transaction using Module Pool. The requirement was to allow warehouse users to post batch material movements with multiple line items in a single transaction — standard MIGO was too complex for this specific use case. I built two screens: Screen 100 for header data with movement type, plant, and posting date; Screen 200 with a table control for batch-wise material entry including material number, quantity, batch, and storage location. I handled all function keys using the OKCODE pattern in PAI — F5 to add a new row to the table control, F8 to validate and post, and standard Back/Cancel for navigation. I created the T-code in SE93 as a dialog transaction type pointing to program SAPMZ_MAT_MOVE Screen 100. For transport, I packaged everything together: the main program, all screen includes (O01, I01, O02, I02), the GUI status from Menu Painter, and the T-code — all in the same Workbench transport request. The transaction has been in Production for 18 months and is used daily by 20 warehouse users.
module-pool-025.interviewScriptHinglish: maine ek custom Material Movement dialog transaction Module Pool use karke develop ki. Requirement yeh thi ki warehouse users ko ek single transaction mein multiple line items ke saath batch material movements post karne ki allow ho — standard MIGO is specific use case ke liye too complex thi. Maine do screens build kiye — Screen 100 header data ke liye movement type, plant aur posting date ke saath; Screen 200 ek table control ke saath batch-wise material entry ke liye including material number, quantity, batch aur storage location. Maine saare function keys PAI mein OKCODE pattern use karke handle kiye — F5 table control mein naya row add karne ke liye, F8 validate aur post karne ke liye, aur standard Back/Cancel navigation ke liye. Maine SE93 mein T-code dialog transaction type ke roop mein create kiya jo program SAPMZ_MAT_MOVE Screen 100 ko point karta tha. Transport ke liye, maine sab kuch together packaged kiya — main program, saare screen includes (O01, I01, O02, I02), Menu Painter se GUI status, aur T-code — sab same Workbench transport request mein. Transaction 18 months se Production mein hai aur daily 20 warehouse users use karte hain.

followupAnswerBank.question: What business requirement justified building a custom Module Pool instead of using standard MIGO?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Standard MIGO ke paas extensive functionality hai but warehouse operators jo repetitive, specific movement types perform karte hain unke liye significant training require karta hai. Business requirement speed ke liye optimized ek simplified transaction tha — sirf us movement type ke liye needed fields, ek go mein 20-30 line items ke bulk entry ke liye table control ke saath. Custom module pool ne training time significantly reduce kiya aur high-volume daily operations ke liye data entry speed improve ki.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Standard MIGO has extensive functionality but requires significant training for warehouse operators who perform repetitive, specific movement types. The business requirement was a simplified transaction optimized for speed — only the fields needed for that movement type, with a table control for bulk entry of 20-30 line items in one go. The custom module pool reduced training time significantly and improved data entry speed for high-volume daily operations.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Build vs buy decision ke liye ROI calculate karo: custom development cost vs productivity gain. 20 users, 200 entries per day, 30 seconds saved per entry = 33 minutes per user per day = 11 hours across team per day. In a month: 220+ hours saved. Development cost recover hota hai 2-3 months mein.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Custom module pool banana sirf isliye ki MIGO 'complex lagta tha' — without proper requirement analysis. Pehle configuration options explore karo (screen personas, MIGO variants), custom development last resort hona chahiye.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Business case comparison:
" MIGO (standard): 50+ fields, multiple tabs, 2-day training
" Custom ZMMAT_MOVE: 6 fields header + table control
"                   30 min training, keyboard optimized

" Custom screen flow:
" Screen 100 (Header):
"   BWART (Movement Type) - mandatory
"   WERKS (Plant)         - defaulted from user profile
"   BUDAT (Posting Date)  - defaulted to today
" Screen 200 (Items - Table Control):
"   MATNR, MENGE, CHARG, LGORT per row
"   F5 = Add row, F8 = Post all items

followupAnswerBank.question: How did you implement the table control for batch entry in Screen 200?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Maine SE51 mein chaar columns ke saath table control design kiya material, quantity, batch, aur storage location ke liye. ABAP program mein, maine isse CONTROLS tc_items TYPE TABLEVIEW USING SCREEN 0200 se declare kiya. PBO mein maine internal table se rows populate karne ke liye LOOP AT lt_items WITH CONTROL tc_items use kiya. PAI mein, same LOOP structure ne row by row user edits MODIFY lt_items FROM ls_item ke saath capture kiye. Rows add karne ke liye, F5 function code lt_items mein blank line append karta tha aur screen refresh karta tha.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: I designed the table control in SE51 with four columns for material, quantity, batch, and storage location. In the ABAP program, I declared it with CONTROLS tc_items TYPE TABLEVIEW USING SCREEN 0200. In PBO I used LOOP AT lt_items WITH CONTROL tc_items to populate rows from the internal table. In PAI, the same LOOP structure captured user edits row by row with MODIFY lt_items FROM ls_item. For adding rows, the F5 function code appended a blank line to lt_items and refreshed the screen.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Table control scrolling: tc_items-lines property set karo to limit visible rows — e.g., 10 rows visible at once. User scroll karta hai neeche — SAP automatically handles scrolling, LOOP mein automatically next set of rows populate hota hai. Developer ko scrolling manually handle nahi karna padta.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: PBO mein LOOP AT WITH CONTROL nahi lagaya — table control blank dikhta hai even if lt_items mein data hai. Common beginner mistake. Always LOOP in both PBO and PAI for table controls.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
CONTROLS: tc_items TYPE TABLEVIEW USING SCREEN 0200.

MODULE pbo_0200 OUTPUT.
  SET PF-STATUS 'ZSTATUS_ITEMS'.
  LOOP AT lt_items INTO ls_item WITH CONTROL tc_items.
    " Each iteration fills one table control row
  ENDLOOP.
ENDMODULE.

MODULE pai_0200 INPUT.
  SAVE_OK_CODE ok_code.
  LOOP AT lt_items INTO ls_item WITH CONTROL tc_items.
    MODIFY lt_items FROM ls_item.  " Capture edits
  ENDLOOP.
  CASE ok_code.
    WHEN 'ADDROW'. APPEND INITIAL LINE TO lt_items.
    WHEN 'POST'.   PERFORM validate_and_post.
    WHEN 'BACK'.   SET SCREEN 100. LEAVE SCREEN.
  ENDCASE.
ENDMODULE.

followupAnswerBank.question: How did you validate data in the table control before posting material movement?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Jab POST function code triggered hota hai, main pehle check karta hoon ki lt_items empty nahi hai. Phir main har row ke through validate karte hue loop karta hoon: material number initial nahi hai, quantity zero se zyada hai, aur agar material batch-managed hai toh batch filled hai. Main material existence ke liye MARA se select karke referential integrity bhi check karta hoon. Agar koi validation fail hoti hai, main MESSAGE type E line number ke saath use karta hoon taaki user exact row identify kar sake. Saari rows pass hone ke baad hi main posting routine call karta hoon.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: When the POST function code is triggered, I first check that lt_items is not empty. Then I loop through each row validating: material number is not initial, quantity is greater than zero, and batch is filled if the material is batch-managed. I also check referential integrity by selecting from MARA for material existence. If any validation fails, I use MESSAGE type E with the line number so the user can identify the exact row. Only after all rows pass validation do I call the posting routine.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Error message with line number: MESSAGE e005(zmsg) WITH 'Row' sy-tabix ': Material is mandatory'. sy-tabix inside LOOP gives current row index. User ne 20 rows enter kiye, row 7 mein matnr missing — exact row number message mein — user directly fix kar sakta hai.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Sirf first row validate karna loop se bahar — baaki rows skip ho jaati hain. Ye partial validation hai. Poora lt_items loop mein validate karo, sab rows check karo before posting.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
FORM validate_and_post.
  IF lt_items IS INITIAL.
    MESSAGE e010(zmsg) WITH 'No line items to post'. RETURN.
  ENDIF.
  LOOP AT lt_items INTO ls_item.
    IF ls_item-matnr IS INITIAL.
      MESSAGE e011(zmsg) WITH 'Row' sy-tabix 'Material missing'.
      RETURN.
    ENDIF.
    IF ls_item-menge <= 0.
      MESSAGE e012(zmsg) WITH 'Row' sy-tabix 'Qty must be > 0'.
      RETURN.
    ENDIF.
    SELECT SINGLE matnr FROM mara WHERE matnr = ls_item-matnr
      INTO @DATA(lv_check).
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
      MESSAGE e013(zmsg) WITH 'Row' sy-tabix 'Invalid material'.
      RETURN.
    ENDIF.
  ENDLOOP.
  PERFORM post_material_movement.  " All validated
ENDFORM.

followupAnswerBank.question: How did you handle the GUI Status for the Material Movement module pool?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Maine SE41 mein do GUI Statuses create kiye — ek Screen 100 header ke liye aur ek Screen 200 items ke liye. Header status mein F5 NEXT function code pe assigned tha, plus standard Back, Exit, aur Cancel. Items status mein F5 ADDROW ke liye, F8 POST ke liye, plus Back, Exit, Cancel tha. Separate statuses context-appropriate toolbars allow karte hain — Post button sirf Screen 200 pe appear hota hai jahaan line items exist karte hain. Har screen ka PBO SET PF-STATUS ke saath appropriate status set karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: I created two GUI Statuses in SE41 — one for Screen 100 header and one for Screen 200 items. The header status had F5 assigned to NEXT function code, plus standard Back, Exit, and Cancel. The items status had F5 for ADDROW, F8 for POST, plus Back, Exit, Cancel. Separate statuses allow context-appropriate toolbars — the Post button only appears on Screen 200 where line items exist. Each screen's PBO sets the appropriate status with SET PF-STATUS.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: GUI Status naming convention: program name + screen indicator. SAPMZ_MAT_MOVE program ke liye: ZSTATUS_HDR for screen 100, ZSTATUS_ITEMS for screen 200. SE41 mein program name enter karo, status name enter karo, create. Function codes columns mein assign karo. Simple but powerful.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Ek hi GUI Status dono screens pe use karna — sab buttons dono screens pe dikhte hain. Screen 100 pe Post button dikhna confusing hai — user click karta hai, kuch nahi hota kyunki PAI mein screen 100 ke module ko POST handle karna nahi aata. Alag status alag screens ke liye.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" SE41 — GUI Status for Screen 100 (Header)
" Status name: ZSTATUS_HDR
" Function codes:
"   F5  → NEXT     (go to screen 200)
"   F3  → BACK
"   F15 → EXIT
"   F12 → CANCEL

" SE41 — GUI Status for Screen 200 (Items)
" Status name: ZSTATUS_ITEMS
" Function codes:
"   F5  → ADDROW   (add blank row to table control)
"   F8  → POST     (validate and post movement)
"   F3  → BACK     (return to screen 100)
"   F12 → CANCEL

MODULE pbo_0100 OUTPUT.
  SET PF-STATUS 'ZSTATUS_HDR'.    " Header toolbar
ENDMODULE.
MODULE pbo_0200 OUTPUT.
  SET PF-STATUS 'ZSTATUS_ITEMS'.  " Items toolbar with POST
ENDMODULE.

followupAnswerBank.question: What did you do after successful posting of material movement in Module Pool?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Successful posting ke baad, main MESSAGE type S use karke material document number ke saath success message display karta hoon. Main phir next entry ke liye lt_items internal table clear karta hoon. Main user ko dialog box ke through choice deta hoon: same header data ke saath another movement post karo (cleared table ke saath screen 200 pe raho), ya new header ke liye screen 100 pe wapas jaao, ya transaction exit karo. Yeh plant aur movement type re-enter kiye bina ek session mein multiple postings allow karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: After successful posting, I display a success message with the material document number using MESSAGE type S. I then clear the lt_items internal table for the next entry. I give the user a choice via a dialog box: post another movement with the same header data (stay on screen 200 with cleared table), or go back to screen 100 for a new header, or exit the transaction. This allows multiple postings in one session without re-entering plant and movement type.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: POPUP_TO_CONFIRM function module use karo for the choice dialog. Isse user ko clean choice milti hai next action ke liye. Better UX than forcing user back to screen 100 after every posting — especially when they have 10 postings of same type to do.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Successful posting ke baad directly EXIT karna — user ko nayi transaction start karni padti hai har posting ke liye. If 10 postings same movement type se hain, 10 baar T-code run karna padta tha. Stay-on-screen with clear items is much better UX.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
FORM post_material_movement.
  " Call BAPI or direct FM for goods movement
  CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE'
    EXPORTING  goodsmvt_header = ls_header
    TABLES     goodsmvt_item   = lt_bapi_items
    IMPORTING  materialdocument = lv_matdoc.
  IF lv_matdoc IS NOT INITIAL.
    CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT'.
    MESSAGE s020(zmsg) WITH 'Document created:' lv_matdoc.
    CLEAR lt_items.        " Ready for next entry
    SET SCREEN 200.        " Stay on screen 200
    LEAVE SCREEN.
  ELSE.
    CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_ROLLBACK'.
    MESSAGE e021(zmsg) WITH 'Posting failed — check errors'.
  ENDIF.
ENDFORM.

followupAnswerBank.question: How would you improve this Module Pool program if you were to redo it today?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Aaj redoing toh, main testability ke liye ABAP classes mein business logic separate karunga — module pool code purely UI event handling hota service class methods call karte hue. Main saari validation logic ke liye ABAP unit tests likhunga. Error handling pehle error pe stop hone ke jagah BAPIRET2 table mein saare errors collect karta aur sab ko ek baar mein display karta. Agar project S/4HANA pe hota, main mobile accessibility ke liye Fiori Elements app evaluate karta kyunki warehouse users increasingly tablets pe kaam karte hain.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: If redoing today, I would separate business logic into ABAP classes for testability — module pool code would be purely UI event handling calling service class methods. I would write ABAP unit tests for all validation logic. Error handling would collect all errors in a BAPIRET2 table and display them all at once rather than stopping at the first error. If the project were on S/4HANA, I would evaluate a Fiori Elements app for mobile accessibility since warehouse users increasingly work on tablets.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Mature developer ki pehchan: wo apni purani code mein improvement opportunities identify kar sakta hai. Interview mein yeh dikhana ki tum sirf kya kiya nahi jaante, balki kya better kar sakte the — ye growth mindset aur technical depth dikhata hai.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Apni purani code ko perfect bolna — 'I would not change anything'. Ye overconfidence dikhata hai ya reflection ki kami. Har project mein improvement areas hote hain. Honestly identify karo aur explain karo why the improvement matters.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Improved approach — OO with service class:
CLASS zcl_mat_move_service DEFINITION.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    METHODS validate_items
      IMPORTING it_items  TYPE ty_t_items
      RETURNING VALUE(rt_errors) TYPE bapiret2_tab.
    METHODS post_movement
      IMPORTING is_header  TYPE ty_header
                it_items   TYPE ty_t_items
      RETURNING VALUE(rv_matdoc) TYPE mblnr.
ENDCLASS.

" PAI module calls service (thin UI layer):
MODULE pai_0200 INPUT.
  SAVE_OK_CODE ok_code.
  IF ok_code = 'POST'.
    DATA(lt_errors) = go_service->validate_items( lt_items ).
    IF lt_errors IS INITIAL.
      gv_matdoc = go_service->post_movement( ls_header, lt_items ).
    ENDIF.
  ENDIF.
ENDMODULE.

followupAnswerBank.question: What were the key challenges in this Module Pool project and how did you solve them?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Key challenges include karte hain scrolling ke dauran table control row capture — sahi LOOP AT WITH CONTROL syntax ke saath properly tc_items control structure use karke solved. Conditional batch validation ek aur challenge tha — saare materials ko batch nahi chahiye, isliye maine per material MARA se batch management indicator check kiya aur conditionally batch entry enforce ki. Performance ko table control se saare material numbers ko range mein collect karke aur saare materials ek baar mein validate karne ke liye ek single SELECT karke optimized kiya bajaye per row ek SELECT.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Key challenges included table control row capture during scrolling — solved by properly using tc_items control structure with correct LOOP AT WITH CONTROL syntax. Conditional batch validation was another challenge — not all materials require batch, so I checked the batch management indicator from MARA per material and conditionally enforced batch entry. Performance was optimized by collecting all material numbers from the table control into a range and doing a single SELECT to validate all materials at once rather than one SELECT per row.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Performance optimization is a great interview talking point. Naive approach: 30 rows = 30 database calls. Optimized approach: 1 database call for all 30. Formula: collect keys into range or internal table, SELECT WHERE matnr IN RANGE lt_matnr_range, store result in hash table, loop over items checking hash table — O(1) per lookup.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Performance optimization nahi karna because 'only 30 rows' — but if 50 users run this simultaneously, 50x30 = 1500 database calls at once. Always optimize database access in loops even for seemingly small volumes.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Performance optimization — bulk material validation:
" Collect all materials from table control
DATA: lt_matnr_range TYPE RANGE OF matnr,
      lt_mara_result TYPE HASHED TABLE OF mara
                     WITH UNIQUE KEY matnr.

LOOP AT lt_items INTO ls_item.
  APPEND VALUE #( sign = 'I' option = 'EQ'
                  low  = ls_item-matnr ) TO lt_matnr_range.
ENDLOOP.

" One SELECT for all materials
SELECT matnr, xchpf FROM mara
  WHERE matnr IN @lt_matnr_range
  INTO TABLE @lt_mara_result.

" Validate using hash table — no DB call per row
LOOP AT lt_items INTO ls_item.
  READ TABLE lt_mara_result WITH KEY matnr = ls_item-matnr
    INTO DATA(ls_mara).
  IF ls_mara-xchpf = 'X' AND ls_item-charg IS INITIAL.
    MESSAGE e015(zmsg) WITH 'Row' sy-tabix 'Batch required'.
  ENDIF.
ENDLOOP.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you answer if the interviewer asks 'Have you worked on Module Pool?' when you have limited experience?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Agar direct production experience limited hai, main honestly bolunga: 'Mera hands-on module pool experience training aur sandbox development ke through hai jahaan maine complete transactions end to end build kiye — screens, PAI/PBO, table controls, SE93 T-code creation. Main full architecture clearly samajhta hoon including transport requirements. Mera framework ka solid understanding given, main confident hoon ki main module pool project effectively deliver kar sakta hoon, aur main kisi bhi production-specific patterns pe jaldi ramp up karunga.' Honest + competence + confidence sahi combination hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: If direct production experience is limited, I would honestly say: 'My hands-on module pool experience is through training and sandbox development where I built complete transactions end to end — screens, PAI/PBO, table controls, SE93 T-code creation. I understand the full architecture clearly including transport requirements. I am confident I can deliver a module pool project effectively given my solid understanding of the framework, and I would ramp up quickly on any production-specific patterns.' Honest + competence + confidence is the right combination.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Agar interview se pehle practice karo: ek complete module pool transaction sandbox mein banao — 2 screens, table control, proper navigation, SE93 T-code. Screenshot lo. Interview mein 'Maine personally build kiya hai sandbox mein' bol sakte ho confidently. Practice = confidence.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Jhoot bolna ki 'haan production mein kiya hai' jab kiya nahi — interviewer follow-up questions se expose ho jaata hai. Aur completely deny karna 'module pool nahi pata' jab concepts clear hain — opportunity miss ho jaati hai.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Even with limited experience, know these key patterns:
" 1. Program structure
PROGRAM zsandbox_demo.
TABLES: ztable.
DATA: ok_code TYPE sy-ucomm.

" 2. PBO pattern
MODULE pbo_0100 OUTPUT.
  SET PF-STATUS 'ZSTATUS'. SET TITLEBAR 'ZTITLE'.
ENDMODULE.

" 3. PAI pattern
MODULE pai_0100 INPUT.
  SAVE_OK_CODE ok_code.
  CASE ok_code.
    WHEN 'SAVE'. PERFORM save_data.
    WHEN 'BACK'. SET SCREEN 0. LEAVE SCREEN.
  ENDCASE.
ENDMODULE.
" Master these 3 patterns = confident module pool answer

module-pool-026.easyUnderstanding: Simple words mein samjho: Urgent support escalation matlab production mein kuch break ho gaya hai — users kaam nahi kar pa rahe, business ruk gaya hai, boss aur client dono call kar rahe hain. Module Pool ke liye specific urgent scenarios: T-code chalane pe 'Screen not found' dump aaya, ya user Save kiya aur kuch nahi hua, ya table control mein data nahi dikh raha. Escalation handle karne ke steps: pehle impact samjho (kitne users affected), phir quick diagnosis (recent kya badla — koi transport gaya kya?), phir fix (hotfix transport ya workaround), phir communicate (stakeholders ko update), phir root cause analysis (permanently fix karo taaki repeat na ho). Calm rehna sabse important hai — panic nahi karte, systematic approach chahiye.
module-pool-026.hinglishMasterExplanation:
Urgent support escalation ka module pool specific framework:

STEP 1 — TRIAGE (5 minutes):
Kaunse users affected hain? All users ya specific user? Specific plant ya all plants? Error message exact kya hai — screenshot lo. Kab se issue start hua? Koi recent change — transport, configuration, user role change?

STEP 2 — QUICK DIAGNOSIS (10-15 minutes):
SM21 — system log dekho. ST22 — ABAP dump analysis. Recent transports STMS mein check karo — last 24 hours mein kya gaya. SE93 — T-code still correctly pointing to right program aur screen? SE51 — screen exist karta hai target system mein?

STEP 3 — IMPACT ASSESSMENT:
Business impact: payment processing ruka, goods movement nahi ho raha, customer order entry blocked? Critical = immediate fix. Non-critical = scheduled fix.

STEP 4 — FIX OPTIONS:
Workaround: kya alternative transaction hai? (e.g., MIGO ki jagah MB1A use kar sakte hain temporarily). Hotfix: development mein fix karo, emergency transport karo — basis team ko involve karo CAB (Change Advisory Board) bypass ke liye agar critical. Rollback: agar recent transport ne break kiya — previous transport re-import karo STMS se.

STEP 5 — COMMUNICATION:
Ticket update karo with current status. Stakeholder email/call: 'Issue identified — cause: XYZ. Fix in progress — ETA: 2 hours. Workaround available: use MIGO for now.' Clear, factual, no panic.

STEP 6 — ROOT CAUSE ANALYSIS (post-fix):
Kyun hua, kaise prevent hoga. Transport checklist update karo. Regression test case add karo.

Module Pool specific escalation triggers:
- 'Dynpro fields: screen 0100 not found' — screen transport missing
- 'CALL_SCREEN' dump — wrong screen number in SET SCREEN call
- 'Table control loop error' — program-screen mismatch after partial transport
- 'Status not found' — GUI Status transport missing
module-pool-026.interviewMeKyaBolnaHai:
Interview Me Bolna Hai:
'For urgent support escalation on a Module Pool issue, I follow a systematic approach. First, I triage — identify how many users are affected, get the exact error message and screenshot, and check what changed recently — specifically recent transports in STMS and any configuration changes. For module pool issues, common root causes are incomplete transports — program transported without screens, or GUI status missing. I use ST22 to analyze any ABAP dump, and SM21 for system log. If a recent transport caused the issue, I assess whether to rollback by re-importing the previous working version or to push a hotfix transport. ST22, SM21, STMS — ye tools mention karna dikhata hai ki tum actually production support jaante ho.'
module-pool-026.codeExamples:
" ============================================================
" Urgent Module Pool Support — Diagnosis Steps
" ============================================================

" STEP 1: Check ABAP dumps in ST22

module-pool-026.quickRevisionNotes:
What: Urgent module pool support follows a fixed triage path — dumps, system log, recent transports, then artefact-by-artefact verification.

Used for:
- P1 incidents like 'transaction does not start' or 'short dump on save'
- Identifying incomplete transports of program, Dynpro, GUI status, or T-code
- Communicating clear status updates to business stakeholders

Key syntax / tables / Tcodes:
- ST22 dumps: DYNPRO_NOT_FOUND, CALL_SCREEN_NOT_FOUND, DYNPRO_FIELD_NOT_FOUND
- SM21 system log, STMS import history
- SE93 T-code, SE51 screen, SE41 status — verify each is present in PRD

Interview keyword: Dump first, transport second, artefact check third — rollback only if no fix in SLA.

followupAnswerBank.question: User reports 'Screen not found' error when running a Module Pool T-code — what do you do?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Ek 'screen not found' ya DYNPRO_NOT_FOUND dump matlab program ek aisa screen number call kar raha hai jo system mein exist nahi karta. Main dump mein exact screen number ke liye ST22 check karta hoon, phir SE51 mein verify karta hoon ki kya woh screen affected system mein exist karta hai. Next main STMS import history check karta hoon yeh identify karne ke liye ki agar ek recent transport ne corresponding Dynpro screen ke bina program import kiya. Fix ya toh missing screen ka emergency transport hai ya previous working transport pe rollback.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: A 'screen not found' or DYNPRO_NOT_FOUND dump means the program is calling a screen number that does not exist in the system. I check ST22 for the exact screen number in the dump, then verify in SE51 whether that screen exists in the affected system. Next I check STMS import history to identify if a recent transport imported the program without the corresponding Dynpro screen. The fix is either an emergency transport of the missing screen or a rollback to the previous working transport.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Real production scenario: urgent transport mein developer ne sirf ABAP program include kiya, Dynpro screen bhool gaya. Production mein T-code chala — DYNPRO_NOT_FOUND dump. 20 users affected. Emergency transport of screen: 45 minutes including QA bypass approval. Lesson: transport checklist mandatory before release.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Seedha SE38 mein program dekh ke guess karna kya galat hai — time waste. ST22 mein dump seedha batata hai kaunsa screen missing hai — exact screen number dump message mein hota hai. ST22 pehle, guessing baad mein.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" ST22 dump analysis for DYNPRO_NOT_FOUND:
" Dump message shows:
" 'Screen 0200 in program SAPMZ_MAT_MOVE not found'
" → Confirms: screen 200 missing in production

" SE51 verification:
" SE51 → Program: SAPMZ_MAT_MOVE → Screen: 0200
" Error: 'Object does not exist' → Transport missing confirmed

" Emergency fix via STMS:
" DEV: Open transport request containing screen 0200
" SE09 → Request → check R3TR DYNP SAPMZ_MAT_MOVE 0200
" → Release → Basis imports to PRD on emergency basis

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you communicate with stakeholders during a Module Pool production issue?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Main ek structured approach use karke communicate karta hoon: 10 minutes ke andar immediate acknowledgment confirm karte hue ki issue investigated ho rahi hai, ek 30-minute update root cause identification aur ETA ke saath, aur ek resolution confirmation brief root cause summary ke saath. Main communication ko factual aur solution-focused rakhta hoon — no blame, business stakeholders ke liye no technical jargon. Main agar available ho toh ek workaround option bhi provide karta hoon taaki fix prepare hone tak business continue kar sake.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: I communicate using a structured approach: immediate acknowledgment within 10 minutes confirming the issue is being investigated, a 30-minute update with root cause identification and ETA, and a resolution confirmation with brief root cause summary. I keep communication factual and solution-focused — no blame, no technical jargon for business stakeholders. I also provide a workaround option if available so business can continue while the fix is prepared.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Stakeholder communication best practice: ek dedicated incident chat group banao — IT leads, business owners, basis team. Sab updates wahan post karo. Alag alag log ko call mat karo — efficient information flow. Ticketing system mein bhi update karo for audit trail.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Technical details business stakeholders ko explain karna — 'screen 200 ka Dynpro transport nahi gaya, R3TR DYNP object STMS mein missing tha.' Business owner ko ye nahi chahiye. Unhe chahiye: impact, ETA, workaround. Technical details IT team ke liye.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Communication template — stakeholder update:

" INITIAL (within 10 min):
" 'Issue: T-code ZMMAT_MOVE unavailable since 14:30
"  Impact: ~20 warehouse users cannot post movements
"  Status: Investigating — update in 30 minutes'

" 30-MIN UPDATE:
" 'Root cause identified: incomplete software deployment
"  Fix: emergency deployment in progress — ETA 16:00
"  Workaround: use transaction MIGO as alternative
"  Next update: on resolution'

" RESOLUTION:
" 'Resolved at 16:10. T-code ZMMAT_MOVE working.
"  Root cause: deployment checklist gap — process updated.
"  No data loss. Post any missed movements now.'

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you perform root cause analysis after resolving a Module Pool production issue?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Resolution ke baad, main symptom se aage true root cause tak pahunchne ke liye 5 Whys technique use karke RCA document karta hoon. Screen missing transport issue ke liye: Why 1 — screen transport request mein nahi tha. Why 2 — developer ne screen add nahi kiya. Why 3 — koi checklist required nahi tha release se pehle screen verification ke liye. Why 4 — transport process formally defined nahi tha. Root cause — mandatory pre-release checklist ki absence. Corrective action: ek module pool transport checklist banao, change management process ke through mandatory karo, aur request release se pehle transport review step add karo.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: After resolution, I document the RCA using the 5 Whys technique to get past the symptom to the true root cause. For the screen missing transport issue: Why 1 — screen was not in transport request. Why 2 — developer did not add screen. Why 3 — no checklist required screen verification before release. Why 4 — transport process was not formally defined. Root cause — absence of a mandatory pre-release checklist. Corrective action: create a module pool transport checklist, make it mandatory via the change management process, and add a transport review step before request release.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: RCA document format: incident summary, timeline, root cause, corrective actions with owners and due dates. Share with team. Review in next sprint retrospective. Most importantly: implement the corrective action — RCA with no action is just paperwork.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: RCA mein sirf symptom fix karna — 'we transported the missing screen.' Ye quick fix hai, root cause nahi. Root cause tha: no process to prevent this. Fix the process, not just the incident.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" RCA Document — Module Pool Transport Issue

" INCIDENT: DYNPRO_NOT_FOUND in PRD — T-code ZMMAT_MOVE
" DATE/TIME: 2026-05-15 14:30 to 16:10 (100 min downtime)
" USERS AFFECTED: 20 warehouse users

" 5 WHYS:
" Why 1: Screen 0200 missing in PRD
" Why 2: Screen not included in transport request
" Why 3: Developer did not verify transport contents
" Why 4: No verification step in release process
" Why 5: Transport checklist not documented/enforced
" ROOT CAUSE: Missing mandatory pre-release checklist

" CORRECTIVE ACTIONS:
" 1. Create Module Pool Transport Checklist (Owner: TL, Due: 1 week)
"    - R3TR PROG, DYNP (all screens), CUAD, TRAN verified
" 2. Code review includes transport review (Owner: TL, Due: 1 week)
" 3. SE09 release requires checklist sign-off (Owner: PM)

followupAnswerBank.question: What tools do you use for Module Pool production support diagnosis?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Module pool production diagnosis ke liye main use karta hoon: ST22 ABAP dump analysis ke liye — yeh exact error, program, screen, aur line number dikhata hai. SM21 system log events ke liye including transport import errors. STMS import history recent changes identify karne ke liye jo issue cause kar sakte hain. SE51 screen existence verify karne ke liye aur SE93 T-code configuration verify karne ke liye. Authorization-related issues ke liye SU53 missing auth object dikhata hai. SM50 performance issues ke liye agar ek module pool process hang ho raha hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: For module pool production diagnosis I use: ST22 for ABAP dump analysis — it shows the exact error, program, screen, and line number. SM21 for system log events including transport import errors. STMS import history to identify recent changes that may have caused the issue. SE51 to verify screen existence and SE93 to verify T-code configuration. For authorization-related issues SU53 shows the missing auth object. SM50 for performance issues if a module pool process is hanging.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Triage sequence in production: ST22 first (2 minutes) — tells you exactly what broke. Then SM21 (3 minutes) — any system-level events around the incident time. Then STMS (5 minutes) — what changed recently. By 10 minutes you know root cause in 90% of module pool cases. Then communicate, then fix.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Seedha SE38 mein program debugging lagana without checking ST22 first — production pe debugger lagana user workflow interrupt karta hai. ST22 se pehle diagnosis karo, debugging sirf last resort hai production mein aur sirf with proper user impact assessment.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Diagnosis tool sequence for Module Pool support:

" 1. ST22 — ABAP Dump (first 2 minutes)
"    Date: today, Time: last 2 hours
"    Find: program SAPMZ_* dumps
"    Key info: error type, screen number, line number

" 2. SM21 — System Log (3-5 minutes)
"    Time range: before/during incident
"    Look for: transport errors, auth failures, RFC errors

" 3. STMS — Transport History (5 minutes)
"    Import Overview → Target system (PRD)
"    → Import history → Last 24-48 hours
"    Identify: what was imported before issue?

" 4. SE51 — Screen verification
"    Program: SAPMZ_MAT_MOVE, Screen: 0200
"    Does screen exist in affected system?

" 5. SE93 — T-code config check
"    T-code: ZMMAT_MOVE → Display
"    Program and screen number correct?

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you handle a situation where a Module Pool fix needs emergency deployment bypassing normal change process?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Emergency deployments business impact, root cause, proposed fix, performed testing, aur rollback plan document karne wale ek emergency change request ke saath CAB approval require karte hain. Approval ke baad, main development mein fix karta hoon, ek quick ATC check run karta hoon, specific broken scenario test karta hoon, transport release karta hoon, aur emergency production import ke liye Basis team ke saath coordinate karta hoon. Import ke baad main immediately smoke testing perform karta hoon. Incident fully documented hota hai aur process improve karne ke liye post-incident meeting mein review kiya jaata hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Emergency deployments require CAB approval with an emergency change request documenting the business impact, root cause, proposed fix, testing performed, and rollback plan. After approval, I fix in development, run a quick ATC check, test the specific broken scenario, release the transport, and coordinate with the Basis team for the emergency production import. After import I perform smoke testing immediately. The incident is documented fully and reviewed in a post-incident meeting to improve the process.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Rollback plan is mandatory in emergency CAB request: 'If the hotfix transport fails or causes new issues, we will re-import transport ZCD109999 (last known good state) — estimated rollback time: 30 minutes.' Without a rollback plan, CAB typically won't approve. Always have plan B ready.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Basis team se production mein manually fix karwane ki request karna — any manual change in production is a serious audit violation. Even in emergency, transport is the only right way. Emergency transport approval process fast hai — usually 30-60 minutes with proper documentation.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Emergency CAB Request — key sections:

" IMPACT STATEMENT:
" 20 users cannot post material movements
" Estimated business loss: X movements pending
" Duration: 100+ minutes if not resolved today

" ROOT CAUSE: Screen 0200 missing from transport
" FIX: Emergency transport ZCD110002 containing
"       R3TR DYNP SAPMZ_MAT_MOVE 0200

" TESTING: Verified screen 0200 in QA — T-code working
" DEV and QA testing: 30 minutes

" ROLLBACK PLAN:
" Re-import ZCD109990 (previous working state)
" Estimated rollback time: 30 minutes
" Business workaround during rollback: use MIGO

" APPROVAL NEEDED FROM: CAB emergency contact

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you avoid module pool production issues in the first place?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Prevention measures mein SE09 release se pehle verified ek mandatory transport checklist saari required object types cover karta hua, transport request contents ka peer review, ATC clean check, saari screen flows aur edge cases cover karne wala QA regression testing, aur business sign-off ke saath scheduled change windows mein production transports restrict karna include hain. Har production import ke baad main immediately ek smoke test run karta hoon success declare karne se pehle basic functionality confirm karne ke liye.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Prevention measures include a mandatory transport checklist verified before SE09 release covering all required object types, peer review of transport request contents, ATC clean check, QA regression testing covering all screen flows and edge cases, and restricting production transports to scheduled change windows with business sign-off. After every production import I immediately run a smoke test to confirm basic functionality before declaring success.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Team practice: before releasing a transport request, developer fills checklist in the ticket: 'Program ✓, Screens 100+200 ✓, GUI Status ✓, T-code ✓, QA tested ✓, ATC clean ✓.' Tech lead reviews checklist before approving STMS import. Two-eye principle prevents single-person transport mistakes.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Friday afternoon transport to production — worst practice. Agar issue aaya, support team weekend pe. Weekend mein fix karna hard. Change management policy: freeze production on Friday afternoons. Monday morning transport — team available for same-day support.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Module Pool Release Checklist (add to change ticket):

" PRE-RELEASE CHECKS:
" [ ] ATC check clean — no critical findings
" [ ] All screens tested in DEV
" [ ] Navigation flow end-to-end verified
" [ ] OKCODE all paths tested
" [ ] Table control (if any) tested with data

" TRANSPORT CONTENTS VERIFIED (SE09):
" [ ] R3TR PROG (main program)
" [ ] R3TR DYNP (each screen: 0100, 0200...)
" [ ] R3TR CUAD (GUI Status)
" [ ] R3TR TRAN (T-code in SE93)

" QA SIGN-OFF:
" [ ] QA regression test complete
" [ ] Business user acceptance done
" [ ] Sign-off from: ____________

" POST-PRODUCTION IMPORT:
" [ ] Smoke test: T-code runs, basic flow works
" [ ] Confirm with affected users

followupAnswerBank.question: What is a workaround and how do you provide one during a Module Pool outage?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Ek workaround business ko fix prepare hone tak continue karne deta hai. Custom module pool transaction ke liye, workaround typically equivalent standard SAP transaction hota hai — for example custom material movement screen ke jagah MIGO, ya custom vendor invoice entry ke jagah FB60. Main step-by-step instructions ke saath workaround document karta hoon aur affected users ko root cause identify karne ke turant baad communicate karta hoon, taaki business operations 15-20 minutes ke andar resume ho sake even agar fix zyada time leta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: A workaround allows business to continue while the fix is prepared. For a custom module pool transaction, the workaround is typically the equivalent standard SAP transaction — for example MIGO instead of a custom material movement screen, or FB60 instead of a custom vendor invoice entry. I document the workaround with step-by-step instructions and communicate it to affected users immediately after identifying the root cause, so business operations can resume within 15-20 minutes even if the fix takes longer.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Workaround documentation should be part of every module pool technical design — 'if this custom transaction is unavailable, use X standard transaction instead.' Keep this in the functional spec or solution document. During outage, go-to document available immediately — no scrambling.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Workaround nahi dena kyunki 'fix mein sirf 1 ghanta lagega.' 1 ghante mein 20 users ka kaam ruka hai — business impact. Hamesha workaround pehle do, fix parallel mein karo. Business continuity first.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Workaround communication template:

" SUBJECT: Workaround for ZMMAT_MOVE issue

" Hi Team,
" T-code ZMMAT_MOVE is temporarily unavailable.
" Please use MIGO as workaround:

" Steps to use MIGO:
" 1. Run T-code MIGO
" 2. Select 'Goods Issue' from action dropdown
" 3. Select 'Other' from reference dropdown
" 4. Enter Plant: 1000 (same as before)
" 5. Enter Movement Type: 261
" 6. Enter materials one by one on line items
" 7. Post

" ZMMAT_MOVE will be restored by 16:00.
" Questions: contact IT helpdesk ext 1234.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you handle a module pool issue where different users experience it differently?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Jab sirf kuch users affected hote hain, main affected versus non-affected user profiles compare karta hoon. Main affected user ke liye SU53 check karta hoon koi authorization failures dekhne ke liye — agar ek module pool PBO mein AUTHORITY-CHECK hai aur user ke paas authorization object nahi hai, screen immediately exit ho jaati hai. Main working aur non-working users ke beech PFCG mein role assignments compare karta hoon. Agar yeh ek data issue hai, main check karta hoon ki affected user ka default plant ya organizational data differs ya nahi. Authorization difference user-specific module pool issues ka sabse common cause hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: When only some users are affected, I compare affected versus non-affected user profiles. I check SU53 for the affected user to see any authorization failures — if a module pool PBO has AUTHORITY-CHECK and the user lacks the authorization object, the screen exits immediately. I compare role assignments in PFCG between working and non-working users. If it is a data issue, I check whether the affected user's default plant or organizational data differs. Authorization difference is the most common cause of user-specific module pool issues.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: SU53 is the fastest tool for user-specific issues: affected user runs the T-code, gets error or sees unexpected behavior. Then runs SU53 immediately after — it shows the last failed authorization check. Exact object, field, and value shown. Go to PFCG, find user's role, add the missing auth object value. User-generated SU53 screenshot — great for helpdesk diagnosis remotely.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Assuming everyone has same authorizations. In SAP, role-based auth means different users have different access. Never assume auth — always check SU53 when issue is user-specific.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" User-specific issue diagnosis:

" Step 1: Affected user runs T-code → gets error
" Step 2: Affected user runs SU53 immediately
" SU53 shows:
" 'Authorization check failed:
"  Object: ZVENDOR_OBJ
"  Field: ACTVT, Value: 03
"  Not in user profile'

" Step 3: Compare roles in PFCG
" Affected user: role Z_VENDOR_BASIC
" Working user: role Z_VENDOR_FULL
" Difference: Z_VENDOR_FULL has ZVENDOR_OBJ with ACTVT 03

" Step 4: Fix — add auth value to Z_VENDOR_BASIC in PFCG
" PFCG → Role Z_VENDOR_BASIC → Authorizations
" → ZVENDOR_OBJ → ACTVT → add value 03
" → Save → Generate profile → User comparison run

module-pool-027.easyUnderstanding: Simple words mein samjho: Functional Specification (FS) ek document hai jo business analyst ya functional consultant likhta hai — 'kya banana hai' uski description. Technical Specification (TS) woh document hai jo developer likhta hai — 'kaise banana hai' ki technical detail. FS to TS conversion ka matlab hai: business requirement ko technical blueprint mein badalna. Ek example: FS kehti hai 'custom screen banao vendor invoices enter karne ke liye, line items ke saath' — TS mein developer likhta hai: Module Pool program SAPLZ_VENDOR_INV, Screen 100 (header: vendor, date, currency), Screen 200 (table control: GL account, amount, tax code), Screen 300 (posting preview), har screen ke PAI modules with validations, transport plan. Functional consultant bolte hain 'kya' — developer bolte hain 'kaise' — TS bridge hai dono ke beech.
module-pool-027.hinglishMasterExplanation:
FS to TS conversion for Module Pool Programming ka detailed breakdown:

FS mein jo hota hai:
- Business process description (vendor invoice entry with line items)
- Screen layout (wireframe level — fields ki list, groupings)
- Validation rules (business language mein — 'vendor must be active', 'amount cannot be negative')
- Navigation flow (user kaise move karega screens ke beech)
- Integration points (posting kaha jaayega — FI, MM, etc.)
- Error scenarios (kya hoga agar validation fail hua)

TS mein developer add karta hai:

1. Program Name: SAPLZ_VENDOR_INV (module pool naming convention — SAPMZ* ya SAPLZ*)

2. Screen Design:
   - Screen 100: Header screen — fields: LIFNR (vendor), BUDAT (invoice date), WAERS (currency), BKTXT (header text). Screen type: Normal. PBO: SET PF-STATUS, SET TITLEBAR, populate vendor defaults. PAI: mandatory field validation.
   - Screen 200: Line item screen — Table control TC_ITEMS with columns: HKONT (GL account), WRBTR (amount), MWSKZ (tax code), ZUONR (assignment). PBO: LOOP AT lt_items WITH CONTROL. PAI: LOOP capture + add row + delete row logic.
   - Screen 300: Posting preview — read-only display of header + items. Confirm posting or go back to correct.

3. GUI Status Design:
   - Screen 100 status ZSTATUS_HDR: F5=Next, F3=Back, F12=Cancel
   - Screen 200 status ZSTATUS_ITEMS: F5=AddRow, F6=DeleteRow, F8=Preview, F3=Back
   - Screen 300 status ZSTATUS_PREVIEW: F8=Post, F3=Back

4. PAI Validation Modules:
   - PAI 100: LIFNR mandatory, LIFNR exists in LFA1, BUDAT not in past (business rule), WAERS valid in TCURC
   - PAI 200: At least one line item, HKONT mandatory per row, WRBTR > 0, MWSKZ valid in T007A
   - PAI 300: Final confirm only — no validation, just POST or BACK

5. Data Model:
   - Global work area for header: gwa_header TYPE ty_vendor_hdr
   - Internal table for items: lt_items TYPE TABLE OF ty_vendor_item
   - Type definitions for custom structures

6. Integration:
   - Use BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST for FI document posting
   - Handle BAPIRET2 return table

7. T-code: ZVI_ENTRY — SE93, dialog transaction, SAPLZ_VENDOR_INV, screen 100

8. Transport plan: program + 3 screens + 3 GUI statuses + T-code

9. Error handling strategy: MESSAGE E in PAI, BAPIRET2 loop for posting errors

10. Authorization: AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT F_BKPF_BUP (posting authorization)
module-pool-027.interviewMeKyaBolnaHai:
Interview Me Bolna Hai:
'When I receive a Functional Specification for a module pool, I first read through the entire document to understand the business process. Then I design the technical solution: I define the program name following naming conventions — for example SAPLZ_VENDOR_INV. I design each screen based on the field groupings in the FS — Screen 100 for header data like vendor, date, currency; Screen 200 with a table control for line items like GL account, amount, and tax code; and Screen 300 as a read-only posting preview. For each screen I define the PBO responsibilities — setting status, titlebar, defaults — and PAI responsibilities — OKCODE handling, validations, navigation. I write the validation logic corresponding to each FS business rule in technical terms — for example FS says vendor must be active, TS says SELECT SINGLE from LFA1 where LIFNR equals input, check sy-subrc, MESSAGE E if not found. I also document the BAPI or FM to be used for the actual business posting, the T-code creation in SE93, and the transport object list. This TS becomes the blueprint for development and serves as documentation for future maintenance.'

Hinglish tip: 'FS to TS conversion answer mein ek specific example use karo — vendor invoice entry example best hai kyunki sab samajh sakte hain. General bolne ki jagah: Screen 100 header, Screen 200 table control, Screen 300 preview — yeh structure clearly batao. Interview mein ye shows ki tum design level pe soch sakte ho, sirf coding level pe nahi.'
module-pool-027.codeExamples:
" FS: 'Custom screen for vendor invoice with line items'
" TS Translation — Module Pool SAPLZ_VENDOR_INV

PROGRAM saplz_vendor_inv MESSAGE-ID zmsg.

" Global data structures (from FS field list)
TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_inv_header,
         lifnr TYPE lifnr,   " Vendor (FS: Vendor field)
         budat TYPE budat,   " Invoice date
         waers TYPE waers,   " Currency
         bktxt TYPE bktxt,   " Header text
END OF ty_inv_header.

TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_inv_item,
         hkont TYPE hkont,   " GL Account (FS: line items)
         wrbtr TYPE wrbtr,   " Amount
         mwskz TYPE mwskz,   " Tax code
         zuonr TYPE zuonr,   " Assignment
END OF ty_inv_item.

DATA: gwa_hdr   TYPE ty_inv_header,
      lt_items  TYPE TABLE OF ty_inv_item,
      ls_item   TYPE ty_inv_item,
      ok_code   TYPE sy-ucomm.

CONTROLS: tc_items TYPE TABLEVIEW USING SCREEN 0200.

" Screen 100 PBO — header screen
MODULE pbo_0100 OUTPUT.
  SET PF-STATUS 'ZSTATUS_HDR'.
  SET TITLEBAR 'ZTITLE_100' WITH 'Vendor Invoice Entry'.
ENDMODULE.
