followupAnswerBank.question: What is the first thing you do when you receive a Functional Specification?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: FS receive karne ke baad, main pehle business process end to end samajhne ke liye complete document read karta hoon. Main ambiguities identify karta hoon — fields mandatory vs optional marked but specified nahi, validation rules jo incomplete hain, missing integration details. Main ek clarification list create karta hoon aur TS likhne se pehle inhe resolve karne ke liye functional consultant ke saath call schedule karta hoon. Ambiguous requirements pe coding rework cause karta hai — upfront clarify karna baad mein 3x time bachata hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: After receiving an FS, I first read the complete document to understand the business process end to end. I identify ambiguities — fields marked as mandatory vs optional but not specified, validation rules that are incomplete, missing integration details. I create a clarification list and schedule a call with the functional consultant to resolve them before writing the TS. Coding on ambiguous requirements causes rework — clarifying upfront saves 3x the time later.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Real project pattern: developer started coding on incomplete FS, built 2 screens, then found out posting should go to FI module via BAPI — not direct table insert. Screen 200 fields changed completely. 2 days of rework. Lesson: complete FS first, then design TS, then code. Never the other way.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: FS receive karna aur immediately coding start karna bina poori padhe — 'I will clarify while coding.' Ye approach har project mein rework causes. Professionals clarify requirements before design, clarify design before coding.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" FS Review Checklist — before writing TS:
" Business process: [ ] Understood end to end
" Screens needed: [ ] Count confirmed (100, 200, 300?)
" Fields per screen: [ ] Complete list with data types?
" Mandatory fields: [ ] Which are mandatory, which optional?
" Validation rules: [ ] Complete rules with exact conditions?
" Navigation flow: [ ] Exact screen-to-screen flow documented?
" Integration: [ ] Which BAPI/FM for posting?
" Authorization: [ ] Which auth objects needed?
" T-code: [ ] Name agreed?
" Clarification log: [ ] All gaps resolved with FC signature

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you translate FS validation rules to Module Pool PAI code?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Main har FS business rule ko precise PAI validation code mein translate karta hoon. For example, FS rule 'vendor must be active' banta hai: SELECT SINGLE LFA1 se sperr aur loevm fields check karte hue — teen separate checks: vendor exists, payment blocked nahi, deletion ke liye marked nahi. Har check ka ek corresponding MESSAGE type E hai user-friendly message ke saath. Amount jaise numeric validations ke liye, main explicit bounds checking add karta hoon. Main PAI code ke alongside FS rule number ko comment ke roop mein document karta hoon taaki future developers code ko wapas requirement tak trace kar sakein.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: I translate each FS business rule into precise PAI validation code. For example, FS rule 'vendor must be active' becomes: SELECT SINGLE from LFA1 checking sperr and loevm fields — three separate checks: vendor exists, not payment blocked, not marked for deletion. Each check has a corresponding MESSAGE type E with a user-friendly message. For numeric validations like amount, I add explicit bounds checking. I document the FS rule number alongside the PAI code as a comment so future developers can trace code back to the requirement.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Traceability is critical for audit: FS rule #12: 'vendor must be active' → PAI module pai_0100, FORM check_vendor_active. Comment in code: '* FS Rule 12: Vendor active check.' Future maintenance mein developer knows exactly why this check is here — do not remove it without updating FS.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: FS rule copy-paste karna as comment but not implementing the actual check. 'TODO: implement vendor active check' in code — this ships to production without the validation. Code review must verify each FS rule is actually implemented.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" FS Rule 12: 'Vendor must be active and not blocked'
" PAI Translation:

FORM check_vendor_active.
  " Check vendor exists in LFA1
  SELECT SINGLE lifnr, sperr, loevm
    FROM lfa1 WHERE lifnr = gwa_hdr-lifnr
    INTO @DATA(ls_lfa1).
  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    MESSAGE e020(zmsg) WITH 'Vendor does not exist'. RETURN.
  ENDIF.
  " Check payment block (FS Rule 12a)
  IF ls_lfa1-sperr = 'X'.
    MESSAGE e021(zmsg) WITH 'Vendor is payment blocked'. RETURN.
  ENDIF.
  " Check deletion flag (FS Rule 12b)
  IF ls_lfa1-loevm = 'X'.
    MESSAGE e022(zmsg) WITH 'Vendor marked for deletion'. RETURN.
  ENDIF.
ENDFORM.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you design Screen 200 table control based on FS line item requirements?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: FS line item fields ke based pe, main SE51 mein corresponding columns ke saath table control design karta hoon: GL account HKONT, numeric display ke liye right-alignment ke saath amount WRBTR, tax code MWSKZ, aur assignment ZUONR. Main in fields ko match karte hue ABAP mein internal table type define karta hoon. CONTROLS declaration ABAP structure ko screen se link karta hai. PBO LOOP AT WITH CONTROL ke through rows populate karta hai, PAI same LOOP ke through edits capture karta hai. Add aur delete row functions ke liye FS requirement GUI Status mein ADDROW aur DELROW function codes mein translate hota hai, PAI mein internal table se appending ya deleting ke through handled.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Based on the FS line item fields, I design the table control in SE51 with corresponding columns: GL account HKONT, amount WRBTR with right-alignment for numeric display, tax code MWSKZ, and assignment ZUONR. I define the internal table type in ABAP matching these fields. The CONTROLS declaration links the ABAP structure to the screen. PBO populates rows via LOOP AT WITH CONTROL, PAI captures edits via the same LOOP. The FS requirement for add and delete row functions translates to ADDROW and DELROW function codes in the GUI Status, handled by appending or deleting from the internal table in PAI.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Column width in table control matters for usability: amount column too narrow = data truncated, user cannot see full amount. Match column width to data element domain length. WRBTR domain = 13 digits + decimal — set column width 15 minimum. Good TS specifies column widths explicitly.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Table control mein all columns as input-capable banate hain default pe — but some columns should be display-only. Example: if GL account is looked up via F4 and cannot be typed freely, set it as output with F4 help only. TS mein specify karo which columns are input vs display.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" TS Design — Screen 200 Table Control
" SE51: Program SAPLZ_VENDOR_INV, Screen 0200
" Table Control: TC_ITEMS, lines: 10 visible
" Columns:
"   HKONT  10 chars  Input (with F4 for GL account)
"   WRBTR  15 chars  Input (right-aligned, amount)
"   MWSKZ   2 chars  Input (with F4 for tax codes)
"   ZUONR  18 chars  Input (free text)

" ABAP program:
CONTROLS: tc_items TYPE TABLEVIEW USING SCREEN 0200.

MODULE pbo_0200 OUTPUT.
  LOOP AT lt_items INTO ls_item WITH CONTROL tc_items.
    " Auto-populates each visible row
  ENDLOOP.
ENDMODULE.

MODULE pai_0200 INPUT.
  SAVE_OK_CODE ok_code.
  LOOP AT lt_items INTO ls_item WITH CONTROL tc_items.
    MODIFY lt_items FROM ls_item INDEX sy-tabix.
  ENDLOOP.
  CASE ok_code.
    WHEN 'ADDROW'. APPEND INITIAL LINE TO lt_items.
    WHEN 'PREVIEW'. SET SCREEN 300. LEAVE SCREEN.
  ENDCASE.
ENDMODULE.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you design Screen 300 posting preview based on FS requirements?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Screen 300 final posting se pehle confirmation ke liye FS requirement ke based pe ek read-only posting preview hai. TS mein main isse display screen ke roop mein define karta hoon — PBO mein saare fields ka screen attribute input=0 hai. PBO header summary populate karta hai aur total invoice amount calculate karne ke liye ek totaling routine call karta hai. PAI sirf do function codes handle karta hai: POST FI document creation ke liye BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST call trigger karta hai, aur BACK corrections ke liye Screen 200 pe return karta hai. Screen 300 pe koi validation needed nahi kyunki saari validation Screens 100 aur 200 pe completed thi.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Screen 300 is a read-only posting preview based on the FS requirement for confirmation before final posting. In TS I define it as a display screen — all fields have screen attribute input=0 in PBO. PBO populates header summary and calls a totaling routine to calculate total invoice amount. PAI handles only two function codes: POST triggers the BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST call for FI document creation, and BACK returns to Screen 200 for corrections. No validation is needed on Screen 300 since all validation was completed on Screens 100 and 200.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Preview screen is very user-friendly — prevents costly posting errors. User sees: Vendor ABC, Date 2026-06-01, 5 line items totaling Rs 1,50,000 — before committing. This business requirement in FS translates directly to technical screen 300 design. One-to-one mapping of business need to technical solution.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Screen 300 mein bhi validations lagana — redundant. Validation is done on 100 and 200. Screen 300 is confirmation only. Duplicate validation causes confusion if validation behavior differs between screens.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Screen 300 — Posting Preview (Read-Only)

MODULE pbo_0300 OUTPUT.
  SET PF-STATUS 'ZSTATUS_PREVIEW'.
  SET TITLEBAR 'ZTITLE_300' WITH 'Posting Preview — Confirm?'.
  " Calculate total
  gv_total = 0.
  LOOP AT lt_items INTO ls_item.
    gv_total = gv_total + ls_item-wrbtr.
  ENDLOOP.
  " Set all fields to display mode
  LOOP AT SCREEN.
    screen-input = 0.  " All fields display-only
    MODIFY SCREEN.
  ENDLOOP.
ENDMODULE.

MODULE pai_0300 INPUT.
  SAVE_OK_CODE ok_code.
  CASE ok_code.
    WHEN 'POST'.
      PERFORM post_vendor_invoice.  " BAPI call
      SET SCREEN 0. LEAVE SCREEN.   " Exit after post
    WHEN 'BACK'.
      SET SCREEN 200. LEAVE SCREEN. " Back to correct
  ENDCASE.
ENDMODULE.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you handle integration with FI module in the Module Pool posting logic?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: FI integration ke liye, TS vendor invoice document create karne ke liye BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST use karne ko specify karta hai. Main gwa_hdr se header data ko BAPI header structure mein map karta hoon company code, vendor invoice ke liye document type KR, posting date, aur currency ke saath. lt_items se line items GL account, debit credit indicator, amount, aur tax code ke saath BAPI item structures mein map hote hain. BAPI call karne ke baad, main RETURN table ke through loop karta hoon — koi message type E ya A matlab failure jisme ROLLBACK chahiye. Success pe main COMMIT call karta hoon aur user ko document number display karta hoon.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: For FI integration, the TS specifies using BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST to create the vendor invoice document. I map the header data from gwa_hdr to the BAPI header structure with company code, document type KR for vendor invoice, posting date, and currency. Line items from lt_items are mapped to BAPI item structures with GL account, debit credit indicator, amount, and tax code. After calling the BAPI, I loop through the RETURN table — any message type E or A means failure requiring ROLLBACK. On success I call COMMIT and display the document number to the user.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST is the correct BAPI for this — not FB01 BDC, not direct table insert. BAPIs are the Clean Core-compliant integration method. Direct table insert into BKPF/BSEG is dangerous and not supported. TS should always specify the exact BAPI name and structure mapping — no ambiguity for the developer.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Using CALL TRANSACTION 'FB60' via BDC for invoice posting — this is fragile, screen-dependent, breaks when FB60 screen changes in SAP support packs. BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST is the right approach — stable API, supported by SAP.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
FORM post_vendor_invoice.
  DATA: ls_doc_header  TYPE bapiache09,
        lt_accountgl   TYPE TABLE OF bapiacgl09,
        lt_accountpayable TYPE TABLE OF bapiacap09,
        lt_return      TYPE TABLE OF bapiret2.
  " Fill header
  ls_doc_header-comp_code  = gv_bukrs.
  ls_doc_header-doc_type   = 'KR'.   " Vendor invoice
  ls_doc_header-pstng_date = gwa_hdr-budat.
  ls_doc_header-doc_date   = gwa_hdr-budat.
  ls_doc_header-currency   = gwa_hdr-waers.
  " Fill line items from lt_items
  LOOP AT lt_items INTO ls_item.
    APPEND VALUE #( itemno_acc = sy-tabix
                    gl_account = ls_item-hkont
                    db_cr_ind  = 'S'
                    currency   = gwa_hdr-waers
                    amt_doccur = ls_item-wrbtr ) TO lt_accountgl.
  ENDLOOP.
  CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST'
    EXPORTING  documentheader = ls_doc_header
    TABLES     accountgl      = lt_accountgl
               return         = lt_return.
  IF line_exists( lt_return[ type = 'E' ] ).
    CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_ROLLBACK'.
    MESSAGE e030(zmsg) WITH 'Posting failed'.
  ELSE.
    CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT' EXPORTING wait = 'X'.
    MESSAGE s031(zmsg) WITH 'Document posted successfully'.
  ENDIF.
ENDFORM.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you document a Technical Specification for a Module Pool program?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Ek complete module pool TS document include karta hai: program name aur type, per screen field lists aur attributes ke saath screen inventory, type definitions ke saath data model, har screen ke liye PBO aur PAI module descriptions, FS reference cross-references ke saath business validation rules, exact BAPI names aur parameter mapping ke saath integration details, T-code configuration details, transport object list, aur key test scenarios. Yeh TS development blueprint aur maintenance documentation dono ke roop mein serve karta hai — ek future developer ko design ko original developer ki need ke bina samajhne mein able hona chahiye.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: A complete module pool TS document includes: program name and type, screen inventory with field lists per screen and attributes, data model with type definitions, PBO and PAI module descriptions for each screen, business validation rules with FS reference cross-references, integration details with exact BAPI names and parameter mapping, T-code configuration details, transport object list, and key test scenarios. This TS serves as both development blueprint and maintenance documentation — a future developer should be able to understand the design without needing the original developer.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: TS document length: typically 15-30 pages for a 3-screen module pool with table control and FI integration. Not too long but complete. Diagrams help: screen navigation flow diagram (boxes and arrows showing screen transitions), screen layout screenshots from SE51. Visual TS is easier to review and approve.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: TS likhna only at code level — 'module pai_0100 INPUT does xyz.' TS should explain the DESIGN not just the code. Anyone should be able to review the TS before a single line of code is written and give approval. Code-level details belong in code comments, not TS.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" TS Document Structure — Module Pool SAPLZ_VENDOR_INV

" 1. OVERVIEW
"    FS Reference: FUNC-SPEC-VND-INV-001 v2.1
"    Business: Vendor invoice entry with line items
"    Scope: Header + items + FI posting

" 2. PROGRAM: SAPLZ_VENDOR_INV
"    Type: Module Pool (Dialog Program)
"    Package: ZFINANCE, Transport: DEVK900789

" 3. SCREENS:
"    Screen 100: Header (Vendor, Date, Currency, Text)
"    Screen 200: Line Items (Table Control TC_ITEMS)
"    Screen 300: Posting Preview (Read-only)

" 4. T-CODE: ZVI_ENTRY
"    Type: Dialog Transaction
"    Program: SAPLZ_VENDOR_INV, Screen: 0100

" 5. INTEGRATION: BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST
"    Document type: KR (Vendor Invoice)
"    Commit: BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT
"    Rollback: BAPI_TRANSACTION_ROLLBACK

followupAnswerBank.question: What naming conventions do you follow for Module Pool programs?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Module pool naming ke liye: program names SAPMZ ya SAPLZ prefix follow karte hain — for example SAPLZ_VENDOR_INV jahaan Z customer namespace indicate karta hai. PBO modules pbo_NNNN aur PAI modules pai_NNNN named hain jahaan NNNN screen number hai. FORM routines ke descriptive names hote hain jaise validate_vendor_data, post_fi_document. Data structures SAP Hungarian notation follow karte hain: internal tables ke liye lt_, work areas ke liye ls_, global variables ke liye gv_, constants ke liye gc_, type definitions ke liye ty_. T-codes customer namespace per Z se start hote hain. Yeh consistency code ko team members ke across readable aur maintainable banati hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: For module pool naming: program names follow SAPMZ or SAPLZ prefix — for example SAPLZ_VENDOR_INV where Z indicates customer namespace. PBO modules are named pbo_NNNN and PAI modules pai_NNNN where NNNN is the screen number. FORM routines have descriptive names like validate_vendor_data, post_fi_document. Data structures follow SAP Hungarian notation: lt_ for internal tables, ls_ for work areas, gv_ for global variables, gc_ for constants, ty_ for type definitions. T-codes start with Z per customer namespace. This consistency makes code readable and maintainable across team members.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Naming convention document project ke start mein create karo — team agreement. Development ke beech convention change karna painful hai — rename everywhere. Usse pehle agree karo TS phase mein: program name, T-code name, screen numbers, module names. TS mein sab document karo.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Program name change karna development ke beech mein — SAPMZ_VEN ko SAPLZ_VENDOR_INVOICE karna. Sab screen includes change hote hain, sab PBO/PAI references update karne padte hain — extensive rework. Finalise naming in TS before any SE38/SE51 work starts.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Naming Convention — Module Pool Standard

" Program:   SAPLZ_VENDOR_INV (SAPL/SAPM + Z + function)
" Screen:    0100, 0200, 0300 (3-digit, leading zero)
" T-code:    ZVI_ENTRY (Z + short function code)

" Modules:
"   MODULE pbo_0100 OUTPUT.   " PBO screen 100
"   MODULE pai_0100 INPUT.    " PAI screen 100

" Data objects:
"   DATA: gv_bukrs  TYPE bukrs,        " gv = global variable
"         gwa_hdr   TYPE ty_inv_header, " gwa = global work area
"         lt_items  TYPE TABLE OF ty_inv_item, " lt = local table
"         ls_item   TYPE ty_inv_item.   " ls = local structure
"   CONSTANTS: gc_doc_type TYPE blart VALUE 'KR'. " gc = global const

" FORM routines:
"   FORM validate_vendor_header.
"   FORM validate_line_items.
"   FORM post_fi_document.
"   FORM check_authorization.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you estimate effort for a Module Pool development based on an FS?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Main module pool effort screen count, table control complexity, integration points, aur validation rules ke based pe estimate karta hoon. Table control aur BAPI integration ke saath ek baseline three-screen module pool typically 3-4 weeks leta hai including unit testing aur transport. Main isse break karta hoon: per screen SE51 mein screen design aur PBO logic, per screen PAI validation logic, integration coding aur error handling, SE41 mein GUI status design, T-code creation, har scenario ka unit testing, aur transport verification. Main unexpected complexity ke liye 20% buffer add karta hoon aur single number ke jagah range ke roop mein present karta hoon.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: I estimate module pool effort based on screen count, table control complexity, integration points, and validation rules. A baseline three-screen module pool with table control and BAPI integration typically takes 3-4 weeks including unit testing and transport. I break it down: screen design in SE51 and PBO logic per screen, PAI validation logic per screen, integration coding and error handling, GUI status design in SE41, T-code creation, unit testing each scenario, and transport verification. I add a 20% buffer for unexpected complexity and present as a range rather than a single number.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Estimation pitfalls: underestimating validation complexity — FS ki 'vendor must be valid' looks simple but translates to 4-5 database checks. Underestimating testing — module pool testing needs all screens, all buttons, all navigation paths, all error scenarios. Testing often takes as long as coding. Always include testing in estimate.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Estimate dena without reading the complete FS. 'Module pool 3 screens — 1 week.' Then finding table control, 15 validations, BAPI integration, 3 authorization objects. Actual effort 4 weeks. Client upset. Always estimate after full FS review.
